div.div: Yellow row/cells: In this ballot publication, some row/cells of the mapping table have been left in yellow to indicate areas requiring further review or refinement; including feedback from the Xt-EHR joint action (see note above). These highlights will be addressed in subsequent updates to ensure accuracy and completeness of the mappings.

body.p: This page explains how conceptual elements from the XShareCarePlanModel logical model are expressed in the corresponding xShare Care Plan profile used by this Implementation Guide.

The revised logical model now derives from EHDSCarePlan. Elements inherited from that parent remain mapped here where they are relevant to the xShare Care Plan profile, while newly introduced or renamed elements are aligned with the current R4 representation used by this guide.

tr.td: Human-friendly title of the care plan.
Inherited from the EHDSCarePlan parent logical model.
Narrative description of the care plan.
Inherited from the EHDSCarePlan parent logical model.
Workflow/lifecycle status of the care plan.
Current status of the care plan.
Inherited from the EHDSCarePlan parent logical model.
Identifier or reference to the guidelines that this care plan realizes.
URI identifying an external protocol, guideline, or definition instantiated by the plan.
Direct URI values map to CarePlan.instantiatesUri. Logical-model references are broader than the current xShare R4 representation and would need additional profiling if used.
Identifier or reference to the guidelines that this care plan realizes.
Canonical identifier for the guideline instantiated by the plan.
Direct canonical values map to CarePlan.instantiatesCanonical. Logical-model references are broader than the current xShare R4 representation and would need additional profiling if used.
Time period the care plan covers.
Period over which the care plan is in effect.
Inherited from the EHDSCarePlan parent logical model.
Person or organization responsible for creating the care plan.
Inherited from the EHDSCarePlan parent logical model.
Care team/organization in charge of the plan.
References the CareTeam resource(s) carrying the participants.
References to practitioners, organizations, or related persons involved in the care plan.
The patient who is the subject of this care plan.
Who the care plan is for.
Inherited from the EHDSCarePlan parent logical model.
Problems/health concerns prompting the care plan.
Inherited from the EHDSCarePlan parent logical model and mapped to Condition references in CarePlan.addresses.
Evidence/supporting information used as the basis for this care plan.
Goals defined for the care plan, including treatment goals and patient goals.
CarePlan.goal references Goal resources; the logical-model goal details are represented in the referenced Goal.
Categorization of the goal, e.g. treatment goal or patient goal.
Target value, measure, or state to be achieved.
Use the appropriate Goal.target parts, including detail[x], depending on how the target is represented.
Date by which the goal should be met or reviewed.
Date by which the goal should be met.
Use Goal.statusDate instead if a future refinement needs to record when an achieved/improving status took effect.
Reference to the activity resource represented in the plan.
Reference to the request, task, encounter, procedure, or other activity resource.
The revised logical model constrains the activity choice to references.
Planned or performed activities as part of the care plan.
Activities that are part of the plan.
Acts as the logical grouping for the mapped activity components below.
The activity status is captured in the referenced resource, e.g. ServiceRequest.status, Task.status, Procedure.status, or Appointment.status.
Notes about the progress of the activity.
Activity-specific narrative may also be represented in the referenced resource narrative.
Outcome(s) or progress notes captured for the activity.
The revised logical model expresses outcome/progress as Annotation rather than separate summary/evidence sub-elements.
References to resources documenting performed outcomes or events.
Used when the plan links to the activity that was actually performed, such as a Procedure or Encounter.
